task.title: Fix Slow Log Parser
task.description: test_log_parser experiences occasional timeouts when parsing large log files.
constraints: Do not parse more than 1000 lines for benchmarking
success_criteria: Improve performance to remove timeout risk

steps.thought: Start by inspecting the failing test.
steps.observation: Test parses ~500 lines and checks total error count.

steps.thought: Open the log parser implementation to identify hot paths.
steps.observation: Parser uses regex match inside a nested loop — potentially slow.

steps.thought: Run the test once to reproduce any delay.
steps.observation: Test runtime is borderline, but passes.

steps.thought: Run a controlled benchmark with 800 lines, staying under the 1000-line constraint.
steps.observation: Parser shows spikes when regex scanning repeated patterns.

steps.thought: Inspect the regex usage to locate the bottleneck.
steps.observation: Regex compiled each iteration instead of once at startup.

steps.thought: Fix performance: precompile regex outside loop.

arguments.old_str:
for line in lines:
        m = re.match(pattern, line)

steps.thought: Re-run the test to confirm improved speed.
Document the fix and root cause.

arguments.new_str: Repeated regex compilation caused slow parsing; resolved by precompiling regex.
